body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 69/2026
Made under: Public Health Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: April 27, 2026

B.C. Reg. 69/2026, deposited April 27, 2026, under thePUBLIC HEALTH ACT[sections 111 and 113]. Order in Council 163/2026, approved and ordered April 27, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with
         the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that (a)	the Reporting Information Affecting Public Health Regulation, B.C. Reg. 167/2018,
         is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 1, and (b)	effective May 15, 2026, the Reporting Information Affecting Public Health
         Regulation is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 2. — J. OSBORNE,Minister of Health; J. WHITESIDE,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Section 3 (4) of the Reporting Information Affecting Public Health Regulation,
         B.C. Reg. 167/2018, is repealed. 1	Section 1 of the Schedule to the Reporting Information Affecting Public Health
         Regulation, B.C. Reg. 167/2018, is amended by adding the following paragraphs: (e.1)	California serogroup viruses; 2	Section 2 of the Schedule is amended by adding the following paragraph: 3	Section 3 of the Schedule is amended (a)	by adding the following paragraphs: (b.1)	respiratory syncytial virus; (c.1)	Rocky Mountain spotted fever; (l.1)	tick-borne relapsing fever;, and (b)	by repealing paragraph (c). Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC adds new diseases to mandatory reporting list for public health
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has updated its Reporting Information Affecting Public Health Regulation to require that certain diseases and viruses be reported to public health authorities. The changes add California serogroup viruses, respiratory syncytial virus (RSV), Rocky Mountain spotted fever, and tick-borne relapsing fever to the list of conditions that must be reported. One old reporting requirement was removed. The new rules took effect on May 15, 2026.
